# Blitz Bridge v1 — Implementation Work Items

**Generated by:** Keaton (Lead)
**Date:** 2026-04-23
**Source:** docs/PRD.md + codebase audit of existing scaffold

---

## Codebase Status at Kickoff

The scaffold is substantially in place. The following are **already implemented**:

| Layer | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| Solution structure | ✅ Done | AppHost, McpServer, ServiceDefaults, Tests project shell |
| MCP server wiring | ✅ Done | HTTP transport, CORS, `/health`, `/mcp` endpoints |
| Configuration model | ⚠️ Partial | `SqlTargetOptions`/`SqlTargetProfile` exists but **missing `AiMode` property** |
| SQL execution service | ✅ Done | Stored proc execution, capabilities query, allowlist validation |
| FRK procedure service | ✅ Done | All 5 tool methods with input validation |
| Result mapper | ✅ Done | FrkResultMapper handles all 5 tool result mappings |
| MCP tool class | ✅ Done | All 5 tools wired with descriptions |
| Request/response models | ✅ Done | All tool request and response DTOs present |
| AppHost / Aspire | ⚠️ Partial | Parameterized secrets for one profile; no `AiMode` env binding |
| Service defaults (OTel) | ✅ Done | Logging, tracing, metrics via standard Aspire template |
| Tests | ❌ Empty | Project exists, no test files |
| Documentation | ❌ Missing | PRD exists; no README setup guide, no operator docs |

---

## Workstreams

### WS-1: Lead — Architecture Hardening & Review Gates (Keaton)

| ID | Task | Depends On | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|------|-----------|---------------------|
| L-1 | Add `AiMode` to `SqlTargetProfile` config model | — | `AiMode` property (int, default 2) exists on `SqlTargetProfile`; config binding verified with unit test |
| L-2 | Enforce `ApplicationIntent=ReadOnly` at startup validation | — | On startup, log a warning if any enabled profile's connection string lacks `ApplicationIntent=ReadOnly`; block startup if `StrictReadOnly` flag is set (future, log-only for v1) |
| L-3 | Add startup profile connectivity validation | L-1 | On boot, test each enabled profile; log degraded status for unreachable targets; expose via health check |
| L-4 | Wire per-profile AiMode into FrkProcedureService | L-1 | When a request doesn't explicitly set AiMode, fall back to the profile-level AiMode default; profile config is the source of truth |
| L-5 | Architecture review gate: Batch 1 sign-off | L-1, L-2, B-1, B-2 | All Batch 1 items pass build + unit tests; no regressions; Keaton approves PR |

### WS-2: Backend — MCP Server Implementation (Fenster)

| ID | Task | Depends On | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|------|-----------|---------------------|
| B-1 | Add connection string sanitization for all log output | — | No log line contains raw connection strings; validated by grep across structured log output in tests |
| B-2 | Implement structured error responses per FR5 | — | Configuration errors return 400; authorization errors return 403; timeout errors return 504; raw SQL errors are never exposed to clients |
| B-3 | Add retry logic with exponential backoff for transient SQL failures | — | Transient `SqlException` codes (e.g., -2, 40613, 40197) retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff; non-transient errors fail immediately |
| B-4 | Implement connection string `ReadOnly` intent enforcement | L-2 | `SqlExecutionService` appends/validates `ApplicationIntent=ReadOnly` on every connection opened; validated in unit test |
| B-5 | Add audit logging for all procedure invocations | B-1 | Every invocation (success + failure) emits structured log: timestamp, profile, procedure, database, status, duration_ms, row_count |
| B-6 | Implement per-profile health check integration | L-3 | `/health` returns per-profile connectivity status; `IHealthCheck` implementation pings each enabled profile |
| B-7 | Add custom OpenTelemetry spans for SQL execution | — | Each `ExecuteStoredProcedureAsync` call creates an Activity span with profile, procedure, database, and duration attributes |
| B-8 | Add custom metrics (invocation count, duration histogram) | B-7 | Meter `BlitzBridge.Diagnostics` with counter `diagnostics.invocations` and histogram `diagnostics.duration_ms` per procedure per profile |

### WS-3: Data & Azure SQL — FRK Integration (Hockney)

| ID | Task | Depends On | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|------|-----------|---------------------|
| D-1 | Validate FRK parameter mapping against latest FRK release | — | Document exact parameter names and types for `sp_Blitz`, `sp_BlitzCache`, `sp_BlitzIndex`, `sp_BlitzFirst`, `sp_BlitzLock`; confirm current code matches |
| D-2 | Verify AiMode parameter behavior across FRK versions | L-1 | Test `@AI = 0`, `@AI = 1`, `@AI = 2` against FRK; document which FRK versions support `@AI`; write compatibility notes |
| D-3 | Create SQL script for minimal FRK test fixture | — | A `.sql` script that installs FRK stub procedures returning canned data; usable in integration tests without a real Azure SQL target |
| D-4 | Document Azure SQL role/permission requirements | — | A markdown file listing minimum SQL permissions for the service account: EXECUTE on FRK procs, VIEW SERVER STATE, etc. |
| D-5 | Validate AllowedDatabases enforcement with cross-database FRK calls | D-1 | Confirm `sp_BlitzIndex @DatabaseName` respects allowlist; edge case: what happens when FRK internally queries `msdb` or `master` |
| D-6 | Resolve open question: database enumeration scope for `target_capabilities` | — | Decision documented: capabilities tool returns only config-level info (not schema counts); Keaton signs off |

### WS-4: Testing & Quality (McManus)

| ID | Task | Depends On | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|------|-----------|---------------------|
| T-1 | Set up test project with xUnit + Moq + FluentAssertions | — | `BlitzBridge.McpServer.Tests` builds and runs `dotnet test` with at least one passing placeholder test |
| T-2 | Unit tests: `SqlTargetProfile` configuration binding | L-1 | Tests verify AiMode default, AllowedProcedures fallback, CommandTimeoutSeconds default, connection string round-trip |
| T-3 | Unit tests: `FrkProcedureService` input validation | T-1 | Tests for every validation path: bad sort order, invalid identifier, out-of-range Top/MaxRows/AiMode/ThresholdMb |
| T-4 | Unit tests: `SqlExecutionService` allowlist enforcement | T-1 | Tests for: unknown profile → error, disabled profile → error, disallowed database → error, disallowed procedure → error |
| T-5 | Unit tests: `FrkResultMapper` output projection | T-1 | Tests for each tool's mapper method with canned DataSet input; verify output shape matches response DTOs |
| T-6 | Unit tests: connection string sanitization | B-1, T-1 | Verify no log output contains connection string substrings; mock ILogger and inspect captured log entries |
| T-7 | Unit tests: structured error response mapping | B-2, T-1 | Verify error types (config, auth, timeout) map to correct HTTP status codes and sanitized messages |
| T-8 | Integration tests: end-to-end MCP tool invocation | D-3, B-2 | Using FRK stub fixture, invoke each MCP tool via HTTP; verify JSON response shape and content |
| T-9 | Integration tests: health check endpoint | B-6 | `/health` returns 200 when profiles are connectable; returns degraded when a profile is unreachable |
| T-10 | Integration tests: retry behavior on transient failure | B-3 | Simulate transient SQL failure; verify 3 retries with backoff; verify non-transient fails immediately |

### WS-5: Documentation & DevRel (Verbal)

| ID | Task | Depends On | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|------|-----------|---------------------|
| DOC-1 | Write README.md with project overview, quickstart, and MCP tool reference | — | README covers: what it does, prerequisites, quickstart with Aspire, tool descriptions, configuration schema |
| DOC-2 | Write operator setup guide (docs/setup-guide.md) | L-1, D-4 | Covers: Azure SQL permissions, FRK installation, profile configuration, Aspire secrets, deployment |
| DOC-3 | Write MCP tool reference (docs/mcp-tools.md) | B-2 | Each tool: purpose, input schema, output schema, error codes, examples |
| DOC-4 | Document AiMode behavior and cost implications | D-2 | Clear explanation of AiMode 0/1/2, when to use each, cost warnings for AiMode 1 |
| DOC-5 | Write CONTRIBUTING.md with dev setup instructions | T-1 | How to clone, build, test, run locally with Aspire; code style notes |
| DOC-6 | Add inline XML docs to public API surface | B-5 | All public classes, methods, and properties in Services/, Models/, Configuration/ have `<summary>` tags |

## Execution Batches

### Batch 1: Foundation (Week 1) — Parallelizable

**Goal:** Close config gaps, establish test infrastructure, validate FRK parameter contracts.

| Task | Owner | Can Parallel? |
|------|-------|---------------|
| L-1: AiMode config property | Keaton | ✅ |
| L-2: ReadOnly startup validation | Keaton | ✅ |
| B-1: Log sanitization | Fenster | ✅ |
| B-2: Structured error responses | Fenster | ✅ |
| T-1: Test project setup | McManus | ✅ |
| T-2: Config binding tests | McManus | After L-1 |
| T-3: Validation unit tests | McManus | After T-1 |
| T-4: Allowlist enforcement tests | McManus | After T-1 |
| D-1: FRK parameter validation | Hockney | ✅ |
| D-3: FRK test fixture script | Hockney | ✅ |
| D-6: Database enumeration decision | Hockney | ✅ |
| DOC-1: README.md | Verbal | ✅ |

**Reviewer gate:** Keaton reviews L-1, L-2, B-1, B-2. McManus reviews T-1–T-4. Build must pass.

**Definition of done:** Config model complete with AiMode. Test project running with ≥15 passing unit tests. FRK parameter contracts documented. README drafted.

---

| Task | Owner | Can Parallel? |
|------|-------|---------------|
| L-3: Startup profile validation | Keaton | After L-1 |
| L-4: Profile-level AiMode wiring | Keaton | After L-1 |
| B-3: Retry logic | Fenster | ✅ |
| B-4: ReadOnly intent enforcement | Fenster | After L-2 |
| B-5: Audit logging | Fenster | After B-1 |
| B-6: Per-profile health check | Fenster | After L-3 |
| B-7: OTel custom spans | Fenster | ✅ |
| B-8: Custom metrics | Fenster | After B-7 |
| T-5: Mapper unit tests | McManus | ✅ |
| T-6: Log sanitization tests | McManus | After B-1 |
| T-7: Error response tests | McManus | After B-2 |
| D-2: AiMode FRK version compat | Hockney | After L-1 |
| D-4: Permission requirements doc | Hockney | ✅ |

**Reviewer gate:** Keaton reviews B-3, B-4, B-5, B-7, B-8. McManus reviews with T-5–T-7 passing. Hockney validates D-2, D-5 findings with Keaton.

**Definition of done:** Retry logic in place. All procedure invocations audited. Health endpoint reports per-profile status. OTel spans and metrics wired. ≥30 passing tests.

---

### Batch 3: Integration & Docs (Week 3)

**Goal:** End-to-end integration tests, remaining docs, final polish.

| Task | Owner | Can Parallel? |
|------|-------|---------------|
| L-5: Architecture review gate | Keaton | After Batch 2 |
| T-8: E2E MCP integration tests | McManus | After D-3, B-2 |
| T-9: Health check integration tests | McManus | After B-6 |
| T-10: Retry integration tests | McManus | After B-3 |
| DOC-3: MCP tool reference | Verbal | After B-2 |
| DOC-4: AiMode documentation | Verbal | After D-2 |
| DOC-5: CONTRIBUTING.md | Verbal | After T-1 |
| DOC-6: XML doc comments | Verbal | After Batch 2 |

**Reviewer gate:** Keaton signs off L-5 (full architecture review). McManus confirms all integration tests pass. Verbal confirms docs are reviewed by Keaton for accuracy.

**Definition of done:** All integration tests pass against FRK stub fixture. All docs complete and reviewed. No open critical issues. Build clean with zero warnings relevant to new code.

---

## Required Sequencing (Critical Path)

```
L-1 (AiMode config) ──→ L-3 (startup validation) ──→ B-6 (health checks) ──→ T-9 (health integration tests)
                    ──→ L-4 (AiMode wiring)       ──→ D-2 (AiMode FRK compat)──→ DOC-4 (AiMode docs)
                    ──→ T-2 (config tests)

B-1 (log sanitization) ──→ B-5 (audit logging) ──→ T-6 (sanitization tests)

B-2 (error responses)  ──→ T-7 (error tests)   ──→ T-8 (E2E integration tests) ──→ DOC-3 (tool reference)

B-7 (OTel spans)       ──→ B-8 (metrics)

D-1 (FRK params)       ──→ D-5 (cross-db edge cases)
D-3 (test fixture)     ──→ T-8 (E2E integration tests)

T-1 (test setup)       ──→ T-2, T-3, T-4, T-5 (all unit tests)
```

---

## Open Decisions Required (Week 1)

| Decision | Owner | Deadline | Impact |
|----------|-------|----------|--------|
| Multi-region targets in v1? | Keaton | Before Batch 1 ends | Affects profile config schema; recommend **no** for v1 (single region) |
| Database enumeration in `target_capabilities`? | Hockney (D-6) | Before Batch 1 ends | Affects capabilities response shape; recommend **config-level info only** |
| Result pagination strategy? | Fenster | Before Batch 2 | Affects response models; recommend **MaxRows cap (already implemented)** as v1 approach |
| Baseline latency targets? | McManus | Before Batch 3 | Affects integration test assertions; recommend **10s p95 for typical queries** |

---

## Kickoff Recommendation — Week 1

**Start everything in Batch 1 simultaneously.** The work is almost entirely parallelizable:

1. **Keaton** closes the config gap (L-1, L-2) — this unblocks half the dependency chain. Should take <1 day.
2. **McManus** stands up the test project (T-1) immediately — this unblocks all unit test work. Begin writing T-3 and T-4 against the existing codebase while waiting for L-1.
3. **Fenster** tackles B-1 (log sanitization) and B-2 (structured errors) in parallel — these are self-contained backend improvements with no config dependencies.
4. **Hockney** validates FRK parameter contracts (D-1) and builds the test fixture script (D-3) — the fixture is critical path for integration tests in Batch 3.
5. **Verbal** starts the README (DOC-1) — enough context exists in the PRD to draft a solid quickstart now.

**Highest-risk item:** D-3 (FRK test fixture). Without it, integration tests in Batch 3 are blocked. Hockney should prioritize this alongside D-1.

**First reviewer gate:** End of Week 1 — Keaton reviews all Batch 1 PRs before Batch 2 begins.
